title: AANA Safety / Adversarial Request Routing HF Diagnostic
public_claim_boundary: This experiment evaluates AANA as an unsafe-request routing, correction, and auditability layer. It does not claim AANA is a full content-moderation model, a production safety guarantee, or a raw agent-performance engine.
objective: Measure whether AANA routes unsafe, adversarial, or ambiguous user requests to refuse, revise, ask, or defer while preserving benign request allow rate.

datasets.purpose: Calibration-only prompt-level toxicity and jailbreak labels for request-routing thresholds.
Calibration-only safe/harmful prompt classification labels for broader request-level safety routing.
Calibration-only harmful behavior prompts for adversarial request coverage.
Calibration-only benign behavior prompts to protect safe allow rate while adding adversarial coverage.
Calibration-only direct harmful request behaviors; test split remains unused for this diagnostic.
Held-out chat-like request-level toxicity and jailbreak validation from a disjoint ToxicChat release.
Held-out adversarial harmful-request stress validation; not used for calibration.
Held-out safe/harmful prompt classification validation after calibration on the train split.

semantic_verifier_policy.required_output: claim-level or request-level safety judgment with category, route, confidence, and no raw harmful text in logs

outputs.raw_text_storage: Raw prompts, model responses, and harmful content are not written to result artifacts; rows contain source ids, hashes, labels, route decisions, category names, signal booleans, AIx scores, and recovery-suggestion presence.
